zh.lifeSummary: 當Florence de la Mare於大約1279年出生在英國艾可布拉德維爾時，她的父親John de la Mare 1st Baron de la Mare30歲，母親Petronilla Dunstanville32歲。她於大約1299年與Philip de Orreby在英國英格蘭素瑞阿申亞德結婚。他們至少是3個兒子的父母。她去世於1344年3月11日享年66歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Florence de la Mare于大约1279年出生在英国艾可布拉德維爾时，她的父亲John de la Mare 1st Baron de la Mare30岁，母亲Petronilla Dunstanville32岁。她于大约1299年与Philip de Orreby在英国英格兰素瑞阿申亞德结婚。他们至少是3个儿子的父母。她去世于1344年3月11日享年66岁。

nameMeanings.name: Mare
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Americo, Cesareo, Fernando, Ines, Javier, Jose, Marcos.  Portuguese Henrique.  Italian Antonio, Gino, Olindo, Silvio, Vito.  Dutch Andries.
Italian: topographic name for someone living by the sea, from mare ‘sea’.

variants.name: Marucci
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Rocco, Antonio, Silvio, Angelo, Carmela, Domenic, Donato, Egidio, Guido, Licinio, Lorenzo, Luciano.
Italian: patronymic or plural form of the personal name Maruccio, a pet form of Mare or Mari .

variants.name: Marelli
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Rocco, Giuseppe, Angelo, Dante.
patronymic or plural form of a diminutive of   Mare  .

variants.name: Mear
meanings: Mear, a field; also from mear or mearh, a horse

nameMeanings.name: Florence
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Medieval form of the Latin masculine name Florentius (a derivative of florens ‘blossoming, flourishing’) and its feminine form Florentia. In the Middle Ages the name was commonly borne by men (as, for example, the historian Florence of Worcester), but it is now exclusively a girl's name. This was revived in the second half of the 19th century, being given in honour of Florence Nightingale ( 1820–1910 ), the founder of modern nursing, who organized a group of nurses to serve in the Crimean War. She herself received the name because she was born in the Italian city of Florence (Latin Florentia, Italian Firenze).

variants.name: Florance
meanings: Medieval form of the Latin masculine name Florentius (a derivative of florens ‘blossoming, flourishing’) and its feminine form Florentia. In the Middle Ages the name was commonly borne by men (as, for example, the historian Florence of Worcester), but it is now exclusively a girl's name. This was revived in the second half of the 19th century, being given in honour of Florence Nightingale ( 1820–1910 ), the founder of modern nursing, who organized a group of nurses to serve in the Crimean War. She herself received the name because she was born in the Italian city of Florence (Latin Florentia, Italian Firenze).

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of the various boys' names with the first syllable Ed-, especially Edward .

variants.name: Edward
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

nameMeanings.name: la
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Florence de la Mare was born about 1279, in Bradwell, Essex, England, her father, John de la Mare 1st Baron de la Mare, was 30 and her mother, Petronilla Dunstanville, was 32. She married Philip de Orreby about 1299, in Ashtead, Surrey, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons. She died on 11 March 1344, at the age of 66.

Elle a épousé Philip de Orreby environ 1299, à Ashtead, Surrey, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils. Elle est décédée le 11 mars 1344, à l’âge de 66 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Florence de la Mare(이)가 대략 1279년 영국 에세스 브라드웰에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John de la Mare 1st Baron de la Mare(은)는 30세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Petronilla Dunstanville(은)는 32세였다. 그녀는 대략 1299년 영국 잉글랜드 수리 애슈테아드에서 Philip de Orreby(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1344년 3월 11일 66세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Florence de la Mare родилась около 1279 г. в местности брадвелл, эссекс, Англия, её отцу по имени John de la Mare 1st Baron de la Mare было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Petronilla Dunstanville, 32. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Philip de Orreby около 1299 г. в местности аштид, сурри, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына.
